game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
{
  "original_game_name": "\uc6b4\ube68\uc874\ub9ce\uac9c",
  "game_name": "Lucky Merge Defense",
  "genre": "Tower Defense",
  "core_mechanics": {
    "description": "Lucky Merge Defense is a grid-based tower defense game where players summon, merge, and place units to defend their base from waves of monsters. All unit acquisitions and upgrades are determined by luck-based systems.",
    "player_actions": [
      {
        "action": "Summon Unit",
        "trigger": "Press 'Space'",
        "details": "Costs in-game currency. A unit is randomly generated (type, rarity, initial stats) based on probabilities. Player enters 'placement mode' to position the unit on an empty grid spot. If no empty grid spots are available, the unit is lost and currency refunded. Upon placement, the unit begins attacking automatically."
      },
      {
        "action": "Place Unit / Select Unit / Merge Units",
        "trigger": "Press 'Shift'",
        "details": "If in 'placement mode' (after summoning), 'Shift' places the hovered unit onto the selected grid spot. If not in placement mode and hovering over an existing unit, 'Shift' selects that unit as the first merge candidate. If a first unit is already selected and 'Shift' is pressed over a second *identical* unit (same type, same rarity) on an adjacent grid spot, the two units are consumed, and a new, higher-tier random unit is generated and placed at the first unit's position. Currency is *not* refunded if units are lost due to full grid or failed merge attempts."
      },
      {
        "action": "Move Cursor",
        "trigger": "Press 'ArrowLeft', 'ArrowRight', 'ArrowUp', 'ArrowDown'",
        "details": "Moves a visual cursor (highlight) across the grid, used for unit placement and selection for merging."
      },
      {
        "action": "Spin Roulette",
        "trigger": "Press 'Z'",
        "details": "Costs in-game currency. Activates a spinning roulette wheel that grants a random reward: additional currency, a temporary powerful unit (e.g., 'Bombardment Cannon' that despawns after 30 seconds), or a temporary global buff (e.g., 'All units +20% Attack for 15 seconds')."
      }
    ],
    "gameplay_rules": [
      "**Unit Summoning**: Each summon costs currency. Unit type (e.g., Archer, Mage, Cannon) and rarity (Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, Legendary) are randomly determined with fixed probabilities. Common units have base stats, higher rarities have significantly improved stats and potentially unique abilities (e.g., AoE for Mage, piercing for Cannon).",
      "**Unit Placement**: Units can only be placed on designated 'tower spots' adjacent to the enemy path on the fixed grid.",
      "**Unit Merging**: Two units must be identical (same type, same rarity) and adjacent to each other to be merged. Merging consumes both units and generates a *new, random unit*. This new unit will always be *one rarity tier higher* than the merged units (e.g., two Common Archers merge into one Uncommon [random type]). The type of the new unit is randomly selected from the pool of available units for that higher rarity tier. Legendary units cannot be merged. Merging is a high-risk, high-reward mechanic.",
      "**Combat**: Units automatically target and attack the closest enemy within their range. Enemies move along a predefined path towards the player's base. Projectiles have travel time. When an enemy is destroyed, it awards currency and points.",
      "**Player Base Health**: The player's base has a fixed health pool. Each enemy that reaches the base reduces its health. If base health reaches zero, the game ends.",
      "**Waves**: Enemies spawn in waves. Each level consists of multiple waves. Completing all waves of a level advances the player to the next level."
    ],
    "state_tracking": [
      "Player current currency (integer).",
      "Player base health (integer).",
      "Current game level (integer).",
      "Current wave number within the level (integer).",
      "List of active units: type, rarity, position (grid coordinates), current health, attack damage, attack range, attack speed, targeting state, unique abilities.",
      "List of active enemies: type, current position (x, y), current health, speed, damage to base, path progress.",
      "List of active projectiles: type, current position, velocity, target enemy, damage.",
      "Roulette cooldown timer (if applicable for spin frequency).",
      "Game timers (wave start delay, enemy spawn timers, buffs duration).",
      "Grid state (which spots are occupied by units, which are empty)."
    ]
  },
  "visual_elements": {
    "game_objects": [
      "Player Base",
      "Enemy Path",
      "Grid Spots (Tower Placement Zones)",
      "Units (Archer, Mage, Cannon - multiple rarities)",
      "Enemies (Basic, Fast, Tanky, Boss - multiple types)",
      "Projectiles",
      "Roulette Wheel",
      "UI Elements (Score, Currency, Base HP, Wave/Level Indicator, Summon Button, Roulette Button, Pause Menu)"
    ],
    "visual_representations": [
      "**Player Base**: A prominent square/rectangle at the end of the path, distinct color (e.g., blue). Flashes red and vibrates slightly when damaged.",
      "**Enemy Path**: A defined winding path rendered as a brown/grey road, clearly visible on the game grid.",
      "**Grid Spots**: Transparent squares on the path's periphery, highlighted green when the cursor hovers over an empty, placeable spot. Red when hovered over an occupied spot.",
      "**Units**: Simple geometric shapes or minimalist sprites. Each unit type has a distinct shape (e.g., Archer: green circle with bow, Mage: purple triangle with staff, Cannon: grey square with barrel). Rarity is indicated by an aura or border color (Common: White, Uncommon: Green, Rare: Blue, Epic: Purple, Legendary: Gold). Small health bar above each unit.",
      "**Enemies**: Simple geometric shapes or minimalist sprites. Each enemy type has a distinct shape/color (e.g., Basic: small red circle, Fast: small yellow triangle, Tanky: large orange square, Boss: large black octagon). Small health bar above each enemy.",
      "**Projectiles**: Small, fast-moving circles or lines corresponding to unit type (e.g., arrow for Archer, magic bolt for Mage, cannonball for Cannon).",
      "**Roulette Wheel**: A circular graphic with distinct segments for different rewards, visually spins rapidly and slows to a stop on the prize.",
      "**UI Elements**: Textual displays for Score (top-right), Currency (bottom-left), Base HP (bottom-right). Wave/Level indicator (top-left). Buttons for Summon and Roulette, clearly labeled. Pause menu with 'Resume', 'Main Menu' options."
    ],
    "animations_effects": [
      "**Unit Attack**: Brief visual flash/recoil for the unit, projectile spawning and trajectory animation.",
      "**Enemy Hit**: Brief color flash (e.g., red tint) on the enemy, health bar update.",
      "**Unit/Enemy Death**: Fade-out effect, small particle burst upon destruction. Currency/points 'pop-up' text for enemies.",
      "**Summoning**: Particle effect (e.g., small star burst) at the unit's spawn location.",
      "**Merging**: Two units dissolve with a particle effect, then a new unit appears with a more prominent particle burst.",
      "**Roulette Spin**: Smooth spinning animation that decelerates to reveal the prize.",
      "**Base Damage**: Base flashes red, screen shake effect (subtle).",
      "**Cursor**: Pulsating highlight effect on the selected grid square.",
      "**Level Transition**: Full-screen overlay with 'Level Complete!' text and level stats."
    ]
  },
  "control_scheme": {
    "ArrowLeft": "Move cursor left on the game grid.",
    "ArrowRight": "Move cursor right on the game grid.",
    "ArrowUp": "Move cursor up on the game grid.",
    "ArrowDown": "Move cursor down on the game grid.",
    "Space": "Initiate unit summon (if enough currency). Enters 'placement mode' with a new random unit.",
    "Shift": "In 'placement mode', place the hovered unit on the selected grid spot. If not in 'placement mode' and cursor is over a unit, 'Shift' selects it for a potential merge. If a unit is already selected, pressing 'Shift' over an *identical adjacent* unit triggers the merge. (If not identical or not adjacent, selection is cleared or ignored.)",
    "Z": "Spin the roulette wheel (if enough currency)."
  },
  "game_states": {
    "main_menu": {
      "name": "MainMenu",
      "description": "The initial screen. Displays game title, 'Start Game' button, 'High Scores' button (optional for initial implementation).",
      "transitions_from": [
        "LevelWin",
        "GameOver",
        "GameWin"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "Playing"
      ]
    },
    "playing": {
      "name": "Playing",
      "description": "The core gameplay loop. Divided into sub-states for wave management.",
      "sub_states": {
        "wave_countdown": {
          "name": "WaveCountdown",
          "description": "Brief pause before a wave starts, allowing player to prepare. Displays 'Wave X Incoming!'",
          "triggers": "Previous wave cleared, or start of a new level."
        },
        "wave_active": {
          "name": "WaveActive",
          "description": "Enemies are actively spawning and moving along the path, units are attacking. Player can summon, merge, and spin roulette.",
          "triggers": "WaveCountdown timer finishes."
        },
        "wave_complete": {
          "name": "WaveComplete",
          "description": "All enemies for the current wave have been defeated, and base health is still above zero. Briefly displays 'Wave X Cleared!'",
          "triggers": "Last enemy of current wave destroyed."
        }
      },
      "transitions_from": [
        "MainMenu",
        "Paused",
        "WaveComplete"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "Paused",
        "LevelWin",
        "GameOver"
      ]
    },
    "paused": {
      "name": "Paused",
      "description": "Game is frozen. An overlay displays 'Paused', with options to 'Resume Game' or 'Return to Main Menu'.",
      "triggers": "Pressing 'Escape' key or clicking a 'Pause' UI button.",
      "transitions_from": [
        "Playing"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "Playing",
        "MainMenu"
      ]
    },
    "level_win": {
      "name": "LevelWin",
      "description": "All waves for the current level have been successfully cleared. Displays 'Level X Complete!', current score, and a 'Next Level' button. If this is the final level, transitions to 'GameWin'.",
      "triggers": "Completion of all waves in the current level with base HP > 0.",
      "transitions_from": [
        "Playing"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "Playing",
        "GameWin",
        "MainMenu"
      ]
    },
    "game_over": {
      "name": "GameOver",
      "description": "Player base health reached zero. Displays 'Game Over!', final score, and options to 'Retry Level' or 'Return to Main Menu'.",
      "triggers": "Player base health drops to 0 or below during any wave.",
      "transitions_from": [
        "Playing"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "Playing",
        "MainMenu"
      ]
    },
    "game_win": {
      "name": "GameWin",
      "description": "Player has successfully completed all levels in the game. Displays 'Congratulations, You Won!', total score, and a 'Return to Main Menu' button.",
      "triggers": "Completion of the final level with base HP > 0.",
      "transitions_from": [
        "LevelWin"
      ],
      "transitions_to": [
        "MainMenu"
      ]
    }
  },
  "win_conditions": "The player successfully completes all waves for all defined levels (e.g., Levels 1 through 3) with the player's base health remaining above zero at the end of the final wave of the final level.",
  "lose_conditions": "The player's base health drops to zero or below at any point during any wave of any level.",
  "scoring_system": {
    "points_awarded": [
      "+10 points per Basic enemy killed.",
      "+25 points per Fast enemy killed.",
      "+50 points per Tanky enemy killed.",
      "+150 points per Boss enemy killed.",
      "+100 points bonus for completing a level.",
      "+1 point for every remaining point of Base HP at the end of a level.",
      "+0.1 points (rounded down) for every remaining unit of Currency at the end of a level."
    ],
    "bonus_multipliers": "No bonus multipliers are implemented for simplification.",
    "high_score_system": "Yes, the game will track the top 5 highest scores, stored locally using browser's localStorage.",
    "score_display": "The current game score will be prominently displayed in the top-right corner of the game screen, formatted as 'SCORE: [current_score]'. During game over or level win states, the final score will be central.",
    "score_affect_gameplay": "No, the score does not directly affect gameplay. It serves purely as a metric for player performance and for tracking high scores."
  },
  "level_system": {
    "level_indicator": "A level indicator will be displayed in the top-left corner of the screen, formatted as 'LEVEL: [current_level] WAVE: [current_wave]/[total_waves_in_level]'.",
    "level_progression": [
      {
        "name": "Level 1: The Rookie Road",
        "difficulty_changes": "Introduces basic enemy types only (Basic, low HP, low speed). Enemy path is simple and straight. Lower enemy density per wave.",
        "waves": 5,
        "base_hp": 100,
        "start_currency": 50,
        "summon_cost": 10,
        "roulette_cost": 5
      },
      {
        "name": "Level 2: The Winding Way",
        "difficulty_changes": "Introduces a new enemy type (Fast, moderate HP, higher speed) alongside Basic enemies. Enemy path is more complex with 1-2 turns. Increased enemy density per wave.",
        "waves": 7,
        "base_hp": 80,
        "start_currency": 75,
        "summon_cost": 12,
        "roulette_cost": 7
      },
      {
        "name": "Level 3: The Crossroads",
        "difficulty_changes": "Introduces a new enemy type (Tanky, high HP, low speed) and a mini-boss enemy in the final wave, in addition to Basic and Fast enemies. Enemy path has multiple turns and possibly a small branching section. Significantly higher enemy density. Currency gain per kill is slightly reduced to encourage more strategic merging/roulette use.",
        "waves": 10,
        "base_hp": 60,
        "start_currency": 100,
        "summon_cost": 15,
        "roulette_cost": 10
      }
    ],
    "advancement_trigger": "The player advances to the next level upon successfully completing all waves of the current level with their base health remaining above zero. A 'Next Level' button will appear on the 'Level Win' screen.",
    "transition_display": "When transitioning between levels, a full-screen overlay will display 'LEVEL [X] COMPLETE!' along with summary stats for the completed level (score, remaining HP, currency) and a prompt 'Press SHIFT to continue to Level [X+1]' (or a 'Next Level' button)."
  },
  "simplifications": [
    "**Unit Rarity & Merging**: The original's 'higher-tier unit' for merging is specified: merging always results in a new unit that is *one rarity tier higher*, but its *type* is randomized. This maintains the 'strategic risk' while simplifying the underlying logic.",
    "**Unit and Enemy Variety**: Initial implementation will focus on 3-4 distinct unit types (Archer, Mage, Cannon) and 4-5 distinct enemy types (Basic, Fast, Tanky, Boss). This limits the number of unique sprites/logic required.",
    "**No Player Leveling System**: The concept of 'leveling up to potentially improve summoning odds or unlock new unit types' is removed. Progression is purely level-based (clearing game levels). Summoning odds are fixed for all game levels.",
    "**Simplified Gacha**: Summoning immediately provides a unit for placement. No complex inventory/collection system for units. Roulette rewards are predefined and static.",
    "**Strictly Single-Player**: All multiplayer features are removed, as is typical for HTML5 casual game conversions.",
    "**Limited Resources**: Currency is the primary in-game resource. No other complex resource management systems are implemented.",
    "**Fixed Pathing**: Enemy paths are predefined per level and static, rather than potentially dynamic or randomized."
  ],
  "implementation_notes": "The game will be implemented using p5.js for all rendering, game logic, state management, and user interface. Collision detection for projectiles and enemies will be handled via simple distance checks or bounding box comparisons within p5.js. Matter.js is not strictly necessary for the core grid-based tower defense mechanics and will be omitted for initial development to maintain simplicity. Scores will be consistently displayed in the top-right corner during gameplay and prominently on game over/win screens. Level progression requires successfully clearing all waves of a level with positive base health before the next level is accessible."
}
game_info.description: Lucky Merge Defense is a grid-based tower defense game where luck meets strategy! Summon random units using currency, merge identical adjacent units to create powerful upgraded defenders, and protect your base from waves of monsters. Every summon and merge is a gamble - will you get the units you need to survive? Use the roulette for game-changing rewards, but manage your resources wisely. Complete all 3 levels to achieve victory!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move cursor on the grid
Space: Summon a random unit (costs currency)
Shift: Place unit / Select unit for merge / Confirm merge
Z: Spin the roulette wheel for random rewards (costs currency)
Enter: Start game from main menu
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to main menu from game over screen
